annotations.label: Algebraic structure

anchor.snippet: an algebraic structure or algebraic system

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes individual algebraic structures via type classes but has no general meta-class for 'algebraic structure' in the universal-algebra sense.

annotations.label: Variety (universal algebra)

anchor.snippet: The collection of all structures of a given type (same operations and same laws) is called a variety

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general framework for varieties in universal algebra.

annotations.label: Rigid motions are associative but not commutative

anchor.snippet: Such moves, formally called rigid motions

annotations.note: This informal motivating example is not stated as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutative operation

anchor.snippet: is commutative if

annotations.note: `CommMagma` axiomatizes a binary operation satisfying commutativity (`Std.Commutative` provides the bare-prop variant).

annotations.label: Associative operation

anchor.snippet: is associative if

annotations.note: `Semigroup` axiomatizes a binary operation satisfying associativity.

annotations.label: Left-distributive operation

anchor.snippet: is left-distributive with respect to another operation

annotations.note: `LeftDistribClass` records the left-distributive law `a*(b+c) = a*b + a*c`.

annotations.label: Right-distributive operation

anchor.snippet: is right-distributive with respect to another operation

annotations.note: `RightDistribClass` records the right-distributive law `(a+b)*c = a*c + b*c`.

annotations.label: Distributive operation

anchor.snippet: is distributive with respect to another operation

annotations.note: `Distrib` bundles both left- and right-distributivity of `*` over `+`.

annotations.label: Distributivity under commutativity

anchor.snippet: left and right distributivity are both equivalent to distributivity

annotations.note: No single lemma asserting that under commutativity left- and right-distributivity are each equivalent to two-sided distributivity was located.

annotations.label: Identity element

anchor.snippet: has an identity element if there is an element

annotations.note: `MulOneClass` axiomatizes a two-sided multiplicative identity (with `One` providing the constant).

annotations.label: Invertible element

anchor.snippet: an element x is invertible if it has an inverse element

annotations.note: `IsUnit x` says `x` has a two-sided multiplicative inverse; `Invertible x` is the data-bearing variant.

annotations.label: Group as algebraic structure

anchor.snippet: a group is an algebraic structure with a binary operation that is associative

annotations.note: `Group` is defined as an associative binary operation with identity and inverses.

annotations.label: Inversion in fields

anchor.snippet: Every nonzero element of a field is invertible

annotations.note: `DivisionRing` (and hence `Field`) builds in the axiom that every nonzero element has a multiplicative inverse.

annotations.label: Set (degenerate structure)

anchor.snippet: a degenerate algebraic structure

annotations.note: A bare set with no operations corresponds to a `Type` in Lean; there is no Mathlib class for the empty-signature algebra.

annotations.label: Group

anchor.snippet: a monoid with a unary operation (inverse), giving rise to inverse elements

annotations.note: `Group` extends `DivInvMonoid` and adds the inverse axiom.

annotations.label: Abelian group

anchor.snippet: a group whose binary operation is commutative

annotations.note: `CommGroup` extends `Group` and `CommMonoid`.

annotations.label: Ring

anchor.snippet: a semiring whose additive monoid is an abelian group

annotations.note: `Ring` extends `Semiring`, `AddCommGroup`, and `AddGroupWithOne`.

annotations.label: Division ring

anchor.snippet: a nontrivial ring in which division by nonzero elements is defined

annotations.note: `DivisionRing` is a nontrivial ring with multiplicative inverses for nonzero elements.

annotations.label: Commutative ring

anchor.snippet: a ring in which the multiplication operation is commutative

annotations.note: `CommRing` is a `Ring` with commutative multiplication.

annotations.label: Field

anchor.snippet: a commutative division ring

annotations.note: `Field` extends `CommRing` and `DivisionRing`.

annotations.label: Complete lattice

anchor.snippet: a lattice in which arbitrary meet and joins exist

annotations.note: `CompleteLattice` provides `sInf` and `sSup` over arbitrary sets.

annotations.label: Bounded lattice

anchor.snippet: a lattice with a greatest element and least element

annotations.note: A bounded lattice is encoded by combining `Lattice` with `BoundedOrder`, which adds `⊤` and `⊥`.

annotations.label: Distributive lattice

anchor.snippet: a lattice in which each of meet and join distributes over the other

annotations.note: `DistribLattice` axiomatizes mutual distributivity of meet and join.

annotations.label: Power set is a distributive lattice

anchor.snippet: A power set under union and intersection forms a distributive lattice

annotations.note: `Set α` is a `BooleanAlgebra`, which entails it is a `DistribLattice` under `∪`/`∩`.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: a complemented distributive lattice

annotations.note: `BooleanAlgebra` is a complemented bounded distributive lattice.

annotations.label: Module

anchor.snippet: an abelian group M and a ring R acting as operators on M

annotations.note: `Module R M` axiomatizes a (semi)ring action on an additive commutative monoid.

annotations.label: Vector space

anchor.snippet: a module where the ring R is a field

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes a vector space as `Module K V` together with `[Field K]` (no separate `VectorSpace` class).

annotations.label: Algebra over a field

anchor.snippet: a module over a field, which also carries a multiplication operation

annotations.note: `Algebra R A` covers algebras over any commutative semiring, specializing to algebras over a field when `R` is a field.

annotations.label: Inner product space

anchor.snippet: a field F and vector space V with a definite bilinear form

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E` axiomatizes a positive-definite (Hermitian) inner product on a normed space.

annotations.label: Topological group

anchor.snippet: a group with a topology compatible with the group operation

annotations.note: `IsTopologicalGroup` asserts continuity of multiplication and inversion.

annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: a topological group with a compatible smooth manifold structure

annotations.note: `LieGroup` axiomatizes a smooth manifold group with smooth multiplication and inversion.

annotations.label: Ordered groups, rings, fields

anchor.snippet: each type of structure with a compatible partial order

annotations.note: Mathlib provides mixin classes such as `IsOrderedRing`, `IsOrderedAddMonoid`, and ordered group instances expressing order compatibility.

annotations.label: Archimedean group

anchor.snippet: a linearly ordered group for which the Archimedean property holds

annotations.note: `Archimedean` is a `Prop` mixin asserting the Archimedean property for an ordered additive monoid.

annotations.label: Topological vector space

anchor.snippet: a vector space whose M has a compatible topology

annotations.note: Mathlib has no single 'TopologicalVectorSpace' class; the notion is assembled from `Module`, `IsTopologicalAddGroup`, and `ContinuousSMul`.

annotations.label: Normed vector space

anchor.snippet: a vector space with a compatible norm

annotations.note: `NormedSpace 𝕜 E` axiomatizes a module with a compatible norm.

annotations.label: Banach space

anchor.snippet: If such a space is complete (as a metric space) then it is called a Banach space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `BanachSpace` class; a Banach space is encoded as `[NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]`.

annotations.label: Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: an inner product space over the real or complex numbers whose inner product gives rise to a Banach space structure

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `HilbertSpace` class; Hilbert spaces are written as `[InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]`.

annotations.label: Von Neumann algebra

anchor.snippet: a *-algebra of operators on a Hilbert space equipped with the weak operator topology

annotations.note: `VonNeumannAlgebra H` is defined as a *-subalgebra of bounded operators equal to its double commutant.

annotations.label: Variety (identities)

anchor.snippet: If all axioms defining a class of algebras are identities, then this class is a variety

annotations.note: Universal-algebra varieties (in Birkhoff's sense) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group term algebra and axioms

anchor.snippet: groups have a signature containing two operators

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the free group `FreeGroup` (the term algebra modulo group identities) but not a general signature/term-algebra framework.

annotations.label: Direct product of fields is not a field

anchor.snippet: the direct product of two fields is not a field

annotations.note: No theorem stating that the binary product of two fields fails to be a field was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Category of an algebraic structure

anchor.snippet: every algebraic structure gives rise to a category

annotations.note: Mathlib defines individual algebraic categories (e.g. `GrpCat`, `RingCat`) but no generic 'category of an arbitrary algebraic structure' construction.

annotations.label: Category of groups

anchor.snippet: the category of groups has all groups as objects and all group homomorphisms as morphisms

annotations.note: `GrpCat` is the bundled category whose objects are groups and morphisms are group homomorphisms.
